title: Files, Descriptors, Locks, and RAII-Like C Patterns · C Contract Brief: Mutex Lock Ownership
summary: A service reload path owns heap buffers, FILE streams, descriptors, sockets, and mutexes across cancellation and errors. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem mutex lock ownership increment in uniform resource-lifetime wrapper. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record which thread owns each lock and guarantee unlock on normal, error, cancellation, and early-return paths.
Use compiler cleanup attributes only behind a documented extension boundary with equivalent standard-C control flow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
